title: 🤖 Copilot Adoption [Edu] [v2]
description: Tracks GitHub Copilot seat provisioning, activation rates, and daily active user trends across the organization.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🤖 Copilot Adoption & Usage

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot Seat Utilisation**

## What it Measures
This dashboard tracks how broadly and actively GitHub Copilot is being used across your organisation. It covers seat allocation, activation rates, and 28-day active usage — giving you a clear picture of Copilot adoption health.

## Why it Matters
Seat utilisation directly affects ROI. Inactive seats represent wasted spend and missed productivity opportunities. Understanding *who* is using Copilot and *how often* helps managers target enablement efforts and coaches identify developers who may need onboarding support.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **Seat Utilisation Rate** | ≥80% active in 28d | <50% — investigate blockers or low awareness |
| **Inactive Seats (>14d)** | Close to 0 | Rising count — reach out to inactive users |
| **Feature Adoption** | Multiple surfaces used | Single surface — expand use-case awareness |

## How to Take Action
- **Low utilisation**: Run Copilot enablement sessions, share use-case demos, create internal champions
- **Many inactive seats**: Survey inactive users to understand blockers (access issues, workflow friction, low awareness)
- **Single-surface usage**: Encourage exploration of Chat, CLI, and PR Summary in addition to code completion

panels.description: Copilot Adoption & Usage — seat utilisation, active users, and feature adoption.

panels.title: Total Seats

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.description: Source: GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats. Point-in-time snapshot. A 403 during sync → 0 rows (check server logs).

panels.title: Active Seats (28d)

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.description: Active = last_activity_at >= NOW()-28d (hardcoded). One interaction in 28 days qualifies. Not aligned to Grafana time range.

panels.title: Seat Utilization Rate (%)
panels.description: Active/Total ratio. 'Active' = rolling NOW()-28d; 'Total' = current snapshot — denominators from different time contexts.

panels.title: Inactive Seats (>14d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL

panels.description: Source: copilot_seats where last_activity_at < NOW()-14d OR IS NULL. 14-day threshold arbitrary — includes vacationing developers.

options.content:
## 📖 Total Seats — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Total count of all rows in copilot_seats at sync time, regardless of activity.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Snapshot, not history** — returns who has a seat right now; no history.
- **Multi-org gap** — multiple GitHub orgs require separate API calls; not auto-aggregated.
- **403 = 0 silently** — sync server logs warning on 403 but continues; table stays empty.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Historical seat assignment changes.
- Billing or cost information per seat.

## 📖 Active Seats (28d) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` → `last_activity_at` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Count of seats where last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'. Any Copilot interaction qualifies.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Broad activity definition** — last_activity_at updated by any interaction, including rejected suggestions.
- **28-day window is hardcoded** — always NOW()-28d, not your Grafana time picker.
- **last_activity_at granularity** — in some API versions this is a date, not datetime; same-day activities indistinguishable.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Frequency of activity within the 28-day window.
- Which Copilot features were used (completions vs chat vs CLI).

## 📖 Seat Utilization Rate (%) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Ratio of active seats (28d) to total seats. Uses PostgreSQL COUNT(*) FILTER syntax.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days')::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1
) AS "Seat Utilization Rate (%)" FROM copilot_seats
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Two different time contexts** — numerator uses rolling NOW()-28d; denominator uses current total. Newly-provisioned seat inflates denominator before user activates.
- **Utilization ≠ value** — 90% utilization with low acceptance rates means Copilot is used but not helpful.
- **Newly provisioned seats deflate rate** — provisioned yesterday counts in denominator immediately.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether highly-utilized seats generate accepted suggestions.
- Which seats are underutilized and why.

## 📖 Inactive Seats (>14d) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Seats where last_activity_at is older than 14 days OR NULL (never active). Useful for reclaim candidates.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **NULL last_activity_at included** — seats never used have NULL, included via OR IS NULL.
- **14-day threshold is arbitrary** — a developer on a 2-week vacation is flagged as inactive.
- **Seat reassignment has billing lag** — GitHub only allows reassignment at end of billing cycle.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Why seats are inactive (vacation, project pause, dissatisfaction).
- Billing impact of reclaiming vs keeping inactive seats.

panels.title: Daily Active Users

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10). TWO-STEP FETCH: API → download_links URL (no auth) → parse NDJSON line-by-line.

panels.title: Last Active Editor Distribution

targets.rawSql:
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.description: Source: copilot_seats.last_activity_editor. Records last-used editor, not primary. Version strings may split same editor into multiple bars.

options.content:
## 📖 Daily Active Users — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)
Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `daily_active_users`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Time series of daily_active_users from the org-level 28-day report. Each row in copilot_org_metrics is one day.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **NDJSON response pattern** — endpoint returns `download_links` envelope. Sync server must: (1) call API, (2) extract `download_links[0].url`, (3) fetch that signed S3 URL WITHOUT GitHub auth headers, (4) parse each line as separate JSON object.
- **28-day rolling window** — /latest always covers last 28 days. Data older than 28 days requires `organization-1-day?day=YYYY-MM-DD`.
- **Unique users per day, not interactions** — a user who made 100 suggestions counts same as one who made 1.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- How many suggestions each active user generated.
- Daily active users by editor, language, or repo.

## 📖 Last Active Editor Distribution — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` → `last_activity_editor` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Bar chart of seat counts grouped by last_activity_editor (e.g., 'vscode', 'neovim', 'jetbrains').

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Last editor, not primary editor** — records most recently used; VS Code daily user who tried CLI once shows as CLI.
- **Editor string includes version** — 'vscode/1.80.0' vs 'vscode' creates separate bars for same editor.
- **NULL excluded** — seats where user has never been active are filtered out.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Primary editor usage across the team.
- Multi-editor users who switch between IDEs.

panels.title: 📖 Last Active Editor Distribution — Learning Guide
Seat Activity Recency

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC

panels.description: Source: copilot_seats ordered by last_activity_at. Point-in-time — no historical trend. plan_type = Individual/Business/Enterprise.

options.content:
## 📖 Seat Activity Recency — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Table of all seat holders sorted by last_activity_at descending, showing login, date, editor, and plan type.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **No activity history** — only the most-recent activity date stored per seat; frequency over time not captured.
- **plan_type field** — shows Copilot plan (Individual / Business / Enterprise); affects feature entitlements.
- **Sync cadence affects recency** — daily syncs are accurate to ±1 day; infrequent syncs produce stale data.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Activity history or trend over time per seat.
- Which specific Copilot features each developer uses.

panels.title: Copilot Seats Loaded

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.description: DATA HEALTH: row count of copilot_seats. If 0, check sync logs for a silent 403 on GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats.

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: sync_jobs. 'success' status does NOT guarantee all resources synced — a silent 403 on any fetcher still produces status='success'.

options.content:
## 📖 Copilot Seats Loaded — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Row count of copilot_seats table — a data health indicator. If 0, the last sync did not load seat data.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Silent 403 failure** — sync server logs warning on 403 but continues; table stays at 0. 'Success' sync ≠ seats loaded.
- **TRUNCATE + INSERT every sync** — unlike PRs/deployments (incremental), copilot_seats fully replaced each sync.
- **Proxy for sync health** — use alongside 'Last Successful Sync'; check server logs if this shows 0.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether seat data is accurate or stale.
- Breakdown by team or repository.

## 📖 Last Successful Sync — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source
Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
MAX(finished_at) from sync_jobs where status='success'. Shows when the last complete sync run finished.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **'Successful' sync ≠ all data loaded** — job completes with 'success' while individual fetchers (e.g., Copilot seats) fail silently. Always check records_synced per resource.
- **Timestamps in UTC** — finished_at stored in UTC; Grafana displays in browser timezone.
- **sync_jobs is append-only** — each sync adds a row; table grows indefinitely.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether all resources were fetched (check records_synced column).
- Failure reason if last job has non-'success' status.
